title: Use `if` with binding instead of `case ... nil ->`
prompt: Refactor this function to use the idiomatic `if var = expr` binding form: `def fetch_user(id) do case Repo.get(User, id) do nil -> :guest; user -> {:user, user} end end`. Rewrite as `if user = Repo.get(User, id), do: {:user, user}, else: :guest`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Sessions do ... end` (with `alias MyApp.{Repo, User}`).
tier_rationale: The dnautics-idiom direction is a small textual swap; easy because it's a direct 1:1 template.
research_citation: dnautics, HN 46752907: 'case functioncall() do nil -> ... end instead of idiomatic if var = functioncall() do else'
